reviews.author: Sandy Smart
reviews.text: Pretty cool experience sitting in the local boats - sampan (hopefully I have spelled it correctly). Nice experience sailing through still waters, while the rowers take you. Pls keep 10000-20000 VND cash with you as they seriously expect you to tip them at the end of each ride. You will see water coconuts on your way, they are coconuts and grow in water, but do not have water in them....LOL You get a chance to put on Vietnamese hats as well during the ride. Nice experience overall

reviews.author: PARTHA BHATTACHARJEE
reviews.text: A great experience boating in the back water canals in country boats. Enjoyed the half an hour boating through the coconut jungle. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Sinead Tripp
reviews.text: This place is an experience, something you would do once and probably wouldnt do again. My main complaint was the tour company we were with did ask for us to tip the rowers 10,000vnd per person on the boat even though our tour covered the hire of the boat and that was fine, in the grand scheme of things 10,000vnd per person isnt much so we were happy to do that and they were providing a service physically rowing us - what put me off though is as we were rowing past other empty boats there were ladies (not rowing our boat or had anything to do with our boat or our tour) screaming at us demanding that we tip them, for what? Im not sure - that I found weird,I had never experienced that travelling Vietnam. And yes they were talking to us because there was no one else around. The 2 people who were physically rowing our boat though were kind and took some photos for us

reviews.author: syamimie nawi
reviews.text: This offers an authentic taste of life in the Mekong Delta. The calm river scenery, orchard visits, and cultural activities like traditional music make it a relaxing and memorable stop. It’s perfect for travelers who enjoy nature and slow-paced exploration. While it doesn’t deliver dramatic sights and some areas can feel a bit touristy, the overall atmosphere and charm easily make it worth a visit.

reviews.author: ASROY BENZ BENZ
reviews.text: GOOD EXPERIENCE SITTING IN THE SAMPAN. BEAUTIFUL VIEW. UNFORTUNATELY, THE ROWERS ARE 2 ELDERLY ROWERS ROWING THE SAMPAN FOR 2KM. ADVISABLE TO GIVE THEM TIPS FOR THEIR HARD WORKS.

reviews.author: Susan M Sipaun
reviews.text: This is a tourist place where they serve you with local fruits and there's local singers singing traditional vietnamese songs. They asked for tips while singing and we pay for the fruits served. It is good place to relax on a hot afternoon. I didn't mind paying.

reviews.author: MAX MAX
reviews.text: Had a nice time as the tour included this place. It must have hard to live there. Obviously the tourists are making there life a good earner now and good luck to them. The people are friendly and the fruits etc were a nice break. The sampan boat was ok around the back of a little local village. Lots of tourists which was nice to see for the locals. Played with a snake and bees which were very friendly. Would recommend

reviews.author: Arunabh palit
reviews.text: Too much crowded, but yes a good experience and worth of photogenic views. But since a major tourist part it has became a fish market type scene.

reviews.author: Angelou Rodrigo
reviews.text: Nothing special in this tour. Will just pass through the river. River is not clean, no narrative whatsoever. Boatmen ask for tips explicitly - they would not even assist you in riding the boat.

reviews.author: TravellerFF
reviews.text: Nice experience! I hope someday locas will learn how to keep trash away from the nature.

reviews.author: Putera Nor Danish
reviews.text: Enjoying my trip here! The Mekong Delta is a serene escape with breathtaking natural beauty. Surrounded by lush greenery and peaceful waterways, it’s an unforgettable experience. The traditional boat ride through the winding canals adds a sense of adventure and immerses you in the local culture. Perfect for nature lovers and those seeking tranquility! 🥰

reviews.author: Rob Hart
reviews.text: Very interesting. Part of the Church Chi Tunnel trip. The sweets are very nice.

reviews.author: Alex Brown
reviews.text: **Mixed Experience with Scenic Views** My visit to Song Tien Tourist Site offered a mix of beautiful scenery and a few challenges. The site is located along the Mekong River, and the views are undoubtedly picturesque. The lush greenery and tranquil river setting create a peaceful atmosphere, making it a pleasant spot to relax and take in the natural beauty of the area. However, the overall experience was somewhat inconsistent. The site felt a bit commercialized, with various vendors and tourist-focused activities that took away from the authenticity of the place. While some of the activities, like boat rides, were enjoyable, others felt a bit forced and geared more towards making a sale than providing a genuine cultural experience. The facilities could use some improvement as well. The bathrooms were not very clean, and some areas of the site seemed a bit neglected. Additionally, the site can get quite crowded, especially during peak hours, which detracted from the serene environment. Despite these issues, the Song Tien Tourist Site does have its moments. The views of the Mekong River are stunning, and if you’re looking to spend some time in nature, it’s still worth a visit. Just be prepared for a more touristy experience than you might expect.

reviews.author: B. Razote
reviews.text: Probably the poster site for Mekong Delta excursions. The ride along the river was one for the books, and I definitely recommend trying it. For a tip of around 10k dong, the boat ride was around 10 minutes, so I think it’s worth it.

reviews.author: Katarina KATULKA
reviews.text: Cruising along the Mekong was a pleasant experience, offering insights into the production of local treats like coconut candies, honey, and various fruits. We visited with our kids, and it was a big hit with everyone.

reviews.author: Son Nguyen Van
reviews.text: It's ready great local experience to travel and know that thr people farming on the islands. But its quite busy sometime, too many tourist

reviews.author: S Shah
reviews.text: Lovely experience, from the boat ride to the hand made coconut candy

reviews.author: Dr. Davin D
reviews.text: Don't let some of the reviews on here keep you away from this amazing place! As an American traveler, I wanted to experience traveling up the Mekong Delta in a sampan, and this was the place to do it! Our guide, Tan, was perfect, allowing us to experience each area as long as we liked, offering snack and drink breaks as well. He was cheerful, and engaging. There were a couple of areas where they asked for tips (as other reviews complained about) but 2,000 -5,000 Dong (about .25 cents USD) was sufficient. We did it all, tried it all, bought some souvenirs and had a ball! Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Samantha Platt
reviews.text: Such a beautiful way to explore the area. The trip in these vintage sampan boats is such a treat and they will even let you have a paddle and wear one of their hats for a photo opportunity. We did this as part of a larger day tour but highly recommend. It is also encouraged to tip your boat person $1USD for each person in your group.

reviews.author: Alexandra Goullier Lhomme
reviews.text: It was terrible ! It felt like a scam. We arrived late afternoon to have some informations to go the day after. They convinced us to go right away. We only wanted to do a boat tour that was supposed to last 30min. The result was a tour of 10min rushing into water while all the other boats were in the way. We waited more than 30minutes (they lost the boat) for that 10min tour. No excuses except that the driver was too fast…There are way better tour than this one which was only looking at our money. In 10min it ruins my very good feeling about Vietnam. Do NOT go there !

reviews.author: Aiswarya v.s
reviews.text: I visited it as part of my mekong delta one day tour package .out guide thinh was very kind enough to explain everything and be with us in all activities .. It's good to visit outskirts and do things differently . Eat their local foods Take a bicycle around their village listen to their folk music and much more !

reviews.author: Jon Franklin
reviews.text: Very crowded Mekong delta tour destination. Was interesting and got to see some different sights but if crowds bother you I would avoid it. That said, we were on a small tour and it was just fine. The small boat ride in a canal was quite the show, dozens upon dozens of small boats with two oarsmen take you a few hundred yards through a small Bamboo lined canal. They will warn you about putting your hands outside the boat as the traffic is heavy and you will get your fingers bashed if you aren't careful! Lunch was just ok, nothing special but you can buy beer so that made things nicer! All in all we rode on 3 different boats and got some nice sight seeing in but as I said, it is very heavily touristed so if staying away from crowds is important to you, I'd recommend looking elsewhere.

reviews.author: K10
reviews.text: Be prepared to asked for tips. Very commercialised tourist spot, keep on selling you the local stuffs. They will bring their coconut ice cream to you, never tell you it’s chargeable until you ask how much it has first bite - scam! Second about the boat ride, forced to pay tip. The male boat driver pushed a female passenger to ask for tip for few times, feel being kidnaped on the boat, no tip cannot berth the boat. Five zero start if I can.

reviews.author: Rey Mark Milloren
reviews.text: It was a nice experience especially when we got to paddle our own boat for a few meters before the end of the river. For those who haven't tried, well there is no harm in trying.

reviews.author: Ema Rimawati
reviews.text: It was fun... The sampan rowing experience on the Mekong River..you will be treated to tropical fruits before riding the sampan, they will lend you a traditional Vietnamese hat during the journey on the river... Don't forget to give a tip to the people who assist in rowing the boat

reviews.author: Adam Wright
reviews.text: Went here as part of the tunnels and Delta river tour. Such an amazing place, with the locals putting on a performance for us and giving us some of their traditional food and drinks. Perfect place to go if you would like to get immersed in the culture!!

reviews.author: Cristina Nannarone Tran
reviews.text: This again touristy but who cares it worth it. These people love to show you their village and life and the ride down the river these men and woman work so hard, you can see it in their hands how hard they work. Enjoy the trees the smells, seeing the little hidden gems inside and tip your boat people trust me that is how they make their living rowing up and down the river countless times a day in the heat.

reviews.author: Dr. D. D. Ebenezer
reviews.text: This was one of the places we visited in the Mekong Delta package which includes island hopping in a boat. Coconut island has coconut sweets made lically. At one place we were served fruits and tea where local artists played and sang traditional Vietnamese songs. We reached it in an electric buggy. A canoe ride is included. Local women rowed it. Our guide, Jacky, Kim Travels, kept us informed and entertained.

reviews.author: sweta mishra
reviews.text: It is a nice place to understand the lives of people living in the mekong river district. Plus a good place to visit if you enjoy nature and boat rides. The tour includes bee keeper visit, plus you can have a taste of honey pollen tee there, you can also see and try coconut candies being made, have a taste of fruits and cultural singing, have alcohol prepared using snakes and Scorpios and have a bicycle ride along the island. Plus you can also have a quite small boat ride along the forest covered river. If you do have time while visiting Ho Chi Min city, I would recommend this tour

reviews.author: Chloe S
reviews.text: The boats are awesome, they tell you about the water coconuts. But they do expect a tip even if you are on a tour, so just make sure you have 10,000-20,000 VND with you.
